title: Brockton mayor served harassment prevention order after incident at graduation ceremony
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Mayor Moises Rodrigues gave a speech at Saturday’s high school graduation that was disrupted by a mother’s cries of, “You know what you did to my daughter!
” Then-mayoral candidate Moises Rodrigues pictured in November 2025.
Pat Greenhouse / The Boston Globe, File By Abby Patkin June 10, 2026 | 1:35 PM 2 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: ‘Devastating’: Ipswich lacrosse team forced to forfeit semifinal over fake graduation cigars, teen says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Despite reassurances from the principal, one Ipswich player said the superintendent chose to bar nine recently-graduated seniors from playing in the state semifinal game.
Ipswich lacrosse players celebrated with their final four trophy after defeating Winthrop on June 5.
The team was forced to forfeit its semifinal matchup against Cohasset.

title: Mike & Patty’s lands at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Flights The popular sandwich shop is in Terminal C.
A breakfast sandwich from Mike & Patty's.
One of Boston’s hottest breakfast sandwich shops has landed at Logan Airport.

title: For many World Cup fans, a trip to Boston is out of reach
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Travel bans, visa restrictions, and high costs threaten to sideline fans hoping to cheer on their national teams at the 2026 tournament.
World Cup 2026 signage on Gillette Stadium, renamed Boston Stadium for the tournament, on June 9, 2026 in Foxborough.
Danielle Parhizkaran / The Boston Globe ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Report: New York Times’ Dianna Russini investigation still ‘weeks away’ from being finished
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots “There’s just a lot to go through, and we obviously want to take our time and be careful doing that.

" Dianna Russini AP Photo/Ben Margot, File According to a report from Page Six, staffers at The Athletic are still awaiting answers from the New York Times’ investigation of the Dianna Russini situation.
Russini had worked at The Athletic until this past April.

title: Injured rock climber rescued from Cannon Cliff after fall
author: John Waller
bullets: Local News "Thankfully, [his] climbing equipment worked well, which likely prevented more serious injuries.
" By John Waller June 10, 2026 | 10:31 AM Less than a minute to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.
A 65-year-old rock climber from Maine was rescued Tuesday after falling on Cannon Cliff in New Hampshire.
